game_name: M.U.S.H.A.: Metallic Uniframe Super Hybrid Armor

sub_pixel_system.notes: Shmup title; sub-pixel precision not documented in available sources

special_mechanics.name: Flight
special_mechanics.description: Player controls a flying mecha vehicle with no grounded movement physics

physics_values.notes: No jump or gravity mechanics applicable; vertical scrolling shooter paradigm

special_mechanics.name: Weapon Powerup System
special_mechanics.description: Main gun powered up by collecting Power Chips; three special weapons available (piercing lasers, fire explosives, rotating shields)

physics_values.notes: Affects gameplay but not movement physics

special_mechanics.name: Drone System
special_mechanics.description: For every three Power Chips, player obtains a drone. Maximum two equipped at once; extras stored. Six attack modes including forward, backward, rotating patterns

physics_values.notes: Affects combat positioning but not core movement physics

special_mechanics.name: Parallax Scrolling
special_mechanics.description: Valley stage utilizes Genesis vertical parallax scrolling; enemies move in/out of screen depth; tiles fall with Doppler effect sound modulation

physics_values.notes: Programmer: Yuichi Toyama; simulates depth perception but does not affect player movement mechanics

tier_notes: Tier 2: Qualitative/empirical data only. MUSHA is a vertically scrolling shooter, not a platformer. Traditional platformer movement parameters (walk speed, jump velocity, gravity, air control) are not applicable. No physics documentation available in provided sources. Game emphasizes fast scrolling action and technical parallax effects rather than character movement granularity.
sources_used: Wikipedia - MUSHA article (gameplay, development, technical features)
